assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 25-33 detail each criterion: (1) Single license filter PASS, (2) Multi-license filter PASS, (3) Invalid license returns 400 PASS, (4) Pagination integration PASS, (5) Response shape unchanged PASS. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' for the corresponding acceptance criterion.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files and 1 new file as specified'. The report identifies the 3 files (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) as matching the task specification.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are report/analysis documents (report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). None of the outputs indicate any code file modifications. The report only contains verification findings and analysis. No evidence of file writes to source code, test files, or configuration files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents findings but does not mention merging the PR. The final line states the report is 'AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr'. No merge commands, merge API calls, or auto-merge instructions appear in any output file. The report is structured as an informational verification report for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` created with 4 integration tests; no existing tests modified or deleted'. Also confirmed in the Test Quality Detail section line 40: 'Test Change Classification: ADDITIVE. `tests/api/package.rs` is a new file (80 lines, 4 test functions). No existing tests modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all expected verdicts: Scope Containment (PASS, line 7), Diff Size (PASS, line 8), Commit Traceability (WARN, line 9) — Intent Alignment domain; Sensitive Patterns (PASS, line 10) — Security domain; CI Status (PASS, line 11), Acceptance Criteria (PASS, line 12), Verification Commands (N/A, line 15) — Correctness domain; Test Quality (PASS, line 13 — combining 'Repetitive Test Detection: PASS', 'Test Documentation: PASS', 'Eval Quality: N/A') and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE, line 14) — Style/Conventions domain. All four domains are represented.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: report.md line 7 provides scope comparison ('2 modified files and 1 new file as specified'). Security: line 10 states 'No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Correctness: lines 25-33 provide per-criterion code-level verification with specific function names, types, SQL clauses, and test assertions for all 5 criteria. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive per-criterion detail with code references. Style/Conventions: lines 37-40 provide test quality assessment with specific counts ('4 test functions exercise distinct behavioral scenarios'), documentation details ('all 4 test functions have /// doc comments'), and classification details ('80 lines, 4 test functions').

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS (4 tests with distinct structures); Test Documentation: PASS (all tests have doc comments); Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 39: 'Eval Quality: N/A. No eval result reviews found on this PR.' Test Quality is PASS based on the other two sub-verdicts, confirming Eval Quality N/A does not affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 39: 'Eval Quality: N/A. No eval result reviews found on this PR.'). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in any output file. Root-Cause Investigation is N/A (line 6: 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'), confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. Both appear at the top of the summary table (lines 5-6), before the domain-specific verdicts (lines 7-15), consistent with being determined independently by the orchestrator rather than by domain sub-agents.
